text: Patient reports dull morning headaches at the back of the head for a few months, fading by lunchtime
transcript_quote: I get these dull headaches at the back of my head some mornings... A few months? They fade by lunchtime.

text: Patient reports bleeding gums when brushing
transcript_quote: my gums bleed when I brush. Nothing dramatic. Just a pink sink, most days.

text: Clinician suspects morning headaches are related to elevated blood pressure and expects them to improve as BP is controlled
transcript_quote: morning headaches and high pressure often travel together. My honest expectation is they fade as the numbers come down.

text: PHQ-2 depression screen score 2 (low, no treatment indicated)
transcript_quote: Those answers put you at a two on the depression screen

text: GAD-7 anxiety screen score 3 (low, no treatment indicated)
transcript_quote: a three on the anxiety scale — both low, not in a range where I'd push treatment

text: AUDIT-C alcohol screen score 3 (below concerning level for men); patient drinks 1-2 beers 2-3 evenings/week
transcript_quote: That scores a three on the alcohol screen — below the level we worry about for men

text: Never smoker; denies tobacco use
transcript_quote: Never. I watched my uncle go from cigarettes.

text: Denies illicit drug use (marijuana, non-prescribed pills)
transcript_quote: No, nothing.

text: Blood pressure elevated at 141/100 mmHg, confirmed on repeat measurement
transcript_quote: Your blood pressure is 141 over 100 — and I rechecked it myself after you'd been sitting a while, same story.

text: Heart rate 100 /min
transcript_quote: Heart rate's 100 today too.

text: Body weight 93.9 kg with BMI 30.21 kg/m2 (obesity)
transcript_quote: Your weight is 93.9 kilos, BMI 30.21, so over the obesity line.

text: Hemoglobin A1c 6.28% consistent with prediabetes
transcript_quote: Your A1c came back at 6.28 percent — that's prediabetes, drifting the wrong direction.

text: Blood glucose 91 mg/dL (normal)
transcript_quote: Glucose today was 91, which is fine day-to-day.

text: Creatinine 1.12 mg/dL and BUN 17.36 mg/dL (kidneys normal)
transcript_quote: Kidneys look good — creatinine 1.12, BUN 17.36.

text: Sodium 139 mmol/L and potassium 3.97 mmol/L
transcript_quote: Sodium 139, potassium 3.97.

text: Diagnosis of essential hypertension
transcript_quote: this is real — essential hypertension, high blood pressure as its own condition.

text: Diagnosis of metabolic syndrome (hypertension, prediabetes, obesity)
transcript_quote: That cluster has a name — metabolic syndrome.

text: Diagnosis of gingivitis; puffy gum line bleeding with light contact, reversible stage

slots.exam: puffy gum line, bleeds with light contact

transcript_quote: That's gingivitis. The good news is it's the reversible stage of gum disease

text: Started hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg oral tablet, one tablet every morning with breakfast
transcript_quote: hydrochlorothiazide, 25 milligrams, one tablet every morning. It's a water pill

text: Counseled on hydrochlorothiazide side effects: increased urination initially, call for lightheadedness on standing or muscle cramps; bloodwork to be rechecked
transcript_quote: If you feel lightheaded when you stand, or you get muscle cramps, call us — it can nudge your potassium and sodium, so we'll recheck bloodwork after you've been on it a while.

text: Vitamin B12 injectable continued unchanged for anemia
transcript_quote: the B12 injections continue unchanged, that's for your anemia and it's doing its job.

text: Medication reconciliation completed; current meds are vitamin B12 injectable and hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg daily, no other supplements
transcript_quote: Reconciled.

text: Counseled on dietary sodium reduction (avoid deli meat, sausage, canned soups, chips) and cooking more at home
transcript_quote: salt is the big lever. Deli meat, sausage, canned soups, chips

text: Counseled to walk 30 minutes most days
transcript_quote: walking: thirty minutes, most days.

text: Enrolled patient in hypertension education program
transcript_quote: I'll enroll you in our hypertension education program

text: Dental referral placed for cleaning and exam (last dental visit 4-5 years ago)
transcript_quote: I'm sending a dental referral today — cleaning and an exam.

text: Counseled on oral hygiene: brush twice daily with soft brush and floss even when bleeding
transcript_quote: Brush twice daily, soft brush, and floss even when it bleeds

text: Administered influenza vaccine (trivalent, split virus, preservative-free) in left arm
transcript_quote: flu vaccine — left arm, I heard? Small pinch... done.

text: Follow-up in about one month for blood pressure recheck; advised to obtain home BP cuff and keep a log (morning, sitting, feet on floor)
transcript_quote: I want to see you back in about a month for a blood pressure recheck. If you can get hold of a home cuff, even better

text: Grief and adjustment: patient was primary caregiver for his mother who died last fall; left work afterward and is not seeking employment

slots.detail: mother died last fall, caregiving, left job

transcript_quote: My mother was sick for a long time — I was her person, day and night, until she passed last fall. After that I just... didn't go back.

text: Not in labor force / currently unemployed, not seeking work
transcript_quote: I stopped working last year and I'm not looking

text: Very low annual income (~a few thousand dollars, ~$4829/yr) from odd jobs; Medicaid insurance
transcript_quote: my own income this year is next to nothing — a few thousand dollars, odd jobs.

text: Housing: living with sister's family, sleeping on sofa bed, 5 people in household; reports feeling safe and has stable housing

slots.detail: living with sister's family, 5 in household, sofa bed

transcript_quote: Her house, her husband, their two kids, and me. Five of us.

text: PRAPARE: family unable to get needed medicine or health care (medical/dental/mental/vision) in past year

slots.detail: unable to get needed care in past year

text: History of migrant/seasonal farm work as main income source at some point in past 2 years
Patient reports fogginess and heavy legs when B12 injections are skipped
transcript_quote: I do notice when I skip — I get foggy and my legs feel like sandbags.

text: Pain severity reported as 1/10
Patient denies feeling depressed and reports sleeping okay; grief symptoms come and go

slots.detail: no depression, grief-related low mood intermittent

transcript_quote: I wouldn't call myself depressed. Tired of my own company, sometimes.

text: Respiratory rate 13 /min
Counseled that if depression/grief worsens, treatment discussion will be revisited
transcript_quote: If it gets heavier, that's a conversation, not a confession.
